nodes.label: AI as test case reviewer and regression testing aid
nodes.label_negative: Manual test case review without AI
nodes.grounding_span: use AI to look over my test cases to see if there's anything I may have missed

nodes.label: AI for meeting summaries and communication
nodes.label_negative: Relying solely on original materials like slideshows
nodes.grounding_span: use it to summarize our retrospective meetings and other adjacent department meetings

nodes.label: AI as safe space for asking questions without social risk
nodes.label_negative: Asking questions in meetings and risking looking silly
nodes.grounding_span: I can ask it to explain things without looking silly at a meeting

nodes.label: AI for repetitive simple tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI handling high-priority or creative tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't feel safe delegating things that are high priority or letting AI decide how to approach test case generation for blockers but more so with repetitive tasks

nodes.label: AI as test case reviewer and regression testing aid
nodes.grounding_span: That combined with my own exploratory testing led to some really fast defect identification

nodes.label: AI as evolving tool requiring human adaptation
nodes.grounding_span: I'd see it as needed to learn how to leverage AI and evolve with it

nodes.label: AI reliability for technical tasks vs. interpersonal tasks
nodes.grounding_span: on the work side of things its great but when asking how to approach another colleague or anything feeling based it can be a bit off base

nodes.label: Quality assurance and defect detection
nodes.grounding_span: led to some really fast defect identification

nodes.label: Understanding and knowledge acquisition
nodes.grounding_span: really get a good understanding in my own time

nodes.label: Professional reputation and social acceptance
nodes.grounding_span: without looking silly at a meeting

nodes.label: Career advancement and adaptability
nodes.grounding_span: there'll always be something I can work on to remain competitive

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for unit testing
nodes.grounding_span: its made me feel extremely satisfied when I used it for test case generation

nodes.label: Cautious delegation based on importance and creativity
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't feel safe delegating things that are high priority or letting AI decide how to approach test case generation for blockers

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: I'd see it as needed to learn how to leverage AI and evolve with it

nodes.label: Recognition of AI limitations in interpersonal contexts
nodes.grounding_span: when asking how to approach another colleague or anything feeling based it can be a bit off base

nodes.label: Risk-based delegation calibration
nodes.grounding_span: when I see opportunities to save time without a big risk of something important being missed I'll delegate lower priority tasks

nodes.label: Verification-oriented approach
nodes.grounding_span: I always double check after though
